name: pyodbc (Python ODBC Database Connector)

auth.notes: ODBC connection strings; supports Windows Auth, SQL Auth, Azure AD

performance.notes: ODBC overhead adds latency vs native drivers; bulk fetches offset this

agent_readiness.notes: DB-API 2.0 ODBC interface for Python. import pyodbc. conn = pyodbc.connect('Driver={ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server};Server=server;Database=db;UID=user;PWD=pass'). cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM table'). rows = cursor.fetchmany(1000). Fast bulk fetch: cursor.fetchall(). Azure SQL, SQL Server, DB2, Oracle, PostgreSQL via ODBC drivers. Windows: Windows Authentication. Parameterized queries: cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM t WHERE id=?', (1,)). Legacy enterprise databases. Pandas integration via pd.read_sql.

gotchas: ODBC driver must be installed separately (not Python package)
No async support — use aioodbc for async
Connection string format varies by database/driver
best_when: SQL Server, Azure SQL, or DB2 connections from Python
Enterprise databases where ODBC is the only connectivity option
Legacy database connectivity in enterprise environments
summary: pyodbc provides Python DB-API 2.0 ODBC connectivity. pyodbc.connect('Driver={ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server};Server=...') for SQL Server, Azure SQL, DB2, Oracle. Parameterized queries, Windows Auth, Azure AD. Requires ODBC driver installed separately. Use for enterprise SQL Server/Azure SQL access — prefer native drivers (psycopg2, asyncpg) for PostgreSQL and MySQL.
